tweets.text: George Hincapie thinks Vanderpoel has the advantage over Pogačar at Roubaix this Sunday. Better team support and more cobble experience might be enough to stop the Slovenian's winning streak.

Modern equipment has transformed Paris-Roubaix. Gone are 25mm tires at 140psi. Now it's 32mm tubeless at 55-65psi with inserts. Hincapie says you rarely see riders walking anymore - the tech is way more forgiving.

Position into cobble sectors isn't about watts - it's about crash risk. "If there's a crash in 20th wheel, the whole peloton's blocked," says Hincapie. Risk management > power output in those opening sectors.
Alpecin's secret weapon for Roubaix: Philipsen. Hincapie says if he makes it to the finale with Vanderpoel and Pogačar, "he's going to be the winner." That sprint could be decisive.

Roubaix breakaways are different. Unlike Flanders where early breaks fade, Roubaix rewards the brave. "Sometimes it's worth that extra effort to make the breakaway and enter sections with 10-15 guys," says Hincapie.
First-year Modern Adventure Pro Cycling brought 30 extra staff just for wheel support at different sectors. The logistics of Roubaix are next level - even for experienced teams.
